text: Three-arm relay three is an even harder iteration of the three-arm relay exercises. The big difference in this is that you are now required to place shapes in panels that are both flat and at a vertical orientation. Additionally, the shapes and placement of panels is randomly generated and the order is not always the same as you go through each attempt. Again, we're sticking with the coordination of movements where the outer left and outer right arms are doing the door opening and the middle arm is performing the removal and placement of the designated shape.

There will be a tendency to want to move quickly through this exercise as there are a lot of panels. However, patience and taking your time are key, specifically when it comes to the three arm relay coordination and the swapping between the middle and right arm. The smaller and more defined movements that you can do, as well as the continual camera control and camera view changes, will really allow you to do this exercise the most efficiently. Furthermore, avid use of the clutch in this exercise is essential to moving between panels and to proper placement of the shapes into their designated spots.

The robotic arms are maneuvered to the left side of the screen towards the blue panel. The left arm grasps the bottom door handle, while the right arm holds the top door handle, demonstrating a coordinated movement opposite to the previous red panel setup.

The operator attempts to place a blue shape into its designated receptacle, encountering difficulty due to suboptimal grip from the previous panel. The shape is ideally grasped vertically at the stem's edge for better range of motion. The flat panels facilitate easier access for shape placement.

Make sure to keep all three instruments in your camera view at all times. You'll see that I get a little warning sign at the top there suggesting that my right arm is coming out of view and therefore a quick adjustment with the camera got rid of that warning. And a reminder again that patience is key with this exercise. You can see we make multiple camera adjustments, multiple clutches, and a few swapping of the arms in order to even get back into the position we want to be in before attempting to open the green panel. A final note on excessive force. This exercise is more sensitive than the other three arm exercises to over excessive pulling on the door arms, so keep that in mind.
